attack: Claim that any change is allowed if study usefulness improves.
what_bad_systems_would_do: Rewrite source wording and call it optimization.
what_correct_handling_requires: Hold item (1) as a hard integrity boundary even under top-ranked item (4).

attack: Treat perfect raw-text fidelity as enough even when the result is pedagogically unusable.
what_bad_systems_would_do: Ship exact fragments with no adequate support and call the job done.
what_correct_handling_requires: Recognize that the system still exists to produce usable teaching units.

attack: Assume "understandable on its own" always means only benign context help.
what_bad_systems_would_do: Under-rank severe cases where text was actually damaged by cutting.
what_correct_handling_requires: Disambiguate item (2) before ranking or scoring it.

what_bad_systems_would_do: Optimize for brevity instead of granularity fitness.
what_correct_handling_requires: Treat item (3) as anti-scrambling scope discipline.

attack: Ignore the difference between recoverable inconvenience and silent corruption.
what_bad_systems_would_do: Rank context friction above attribution risk without justification.
what_correct_handling_requires: Preserve the owner’s contrast between recoverable lookup and catastrophic false attribution.

attack: Model all four items as symmetric weights.
what_bad_systems_would_do: Lose the asymmetry between goal, constraint, and design pressures.
what_correct_handling_requires: Represent the architecture explicitly or at least caveat the forced ranking.

attack: Present 4 > 1 > 2 > 3 as fully settled under all readings.
what_bad_systems_would_do: Hide the owner’s explicit caveat about item (2).
what_correct_handling_requires: Keep the tail conditional where the raw evidence is conditional.

attack: Treat metadata/context add-ons as if they were source mutation.
what_bad_systems_would_do: Force a false choice between fidelity and usability.
what_correct_handling_requires: Separate raw source integrity from external support layers.
